zone_6b_notes: Hardy perennial in Zone 6b — one of the first plants to emerge in Farmington in late February or early March. Dies back in summer heat then returns in fall; cut back hard in August to encourage fresh growth. Beautiful purple-pink edible flowers in May are deer-resistant and attract pollinators. Divide clumps every 3 years to maintain vigor. Snip leaves to about 1 inch from base — plants will regrow quickly. Self-seeds prolifically if flowers are allowed to go to seed; deadhead after bloom to control spread.

planting.best_planting_time: Direct sow in early spring or plant divisions at any time during growing season
